# Break It Walkthrough
## Intro
Welcome to the Break It challenge, here is the link to the [room](https://tryhackme.com/room/breakit) on TryHackMe.

This is a challenge about decoding a series of strings that are encoded in multiple ways.

The room is divided into 3 tasks, here is the index:
- [[#Bases]]
- [[#Base and Cipher]]
- [[#Base, Cipher and Bit Shift]]

This time is not needed to start any machine or download any file.
Let's begin!

<br/>
<br/>

## The Challenge
To prepare for what we'll be doing we can open *CyberChef* in our browser, as it will be our best friend here.
Otherwise if you prefer the terminal you can use tools such as `base64`, `xxd` and so on.
Example:
```
echo "BASE64_ENCODED_STRING_HERE" | base64 -d
```

If we find multiple layers of encoding we can simply add another pipe, es. `|base64 -d | base32 -d`.

### Bases
In this first part we'll deal with strings of different levels of encoding

1. **[Super easy] `MVQXG6K7MJQXGZJTGI======`** <br>
	This is a base32 string: <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

2. **[Easy] `TVJYWEtZVE1NVlBXRVlMVE1WWlE9PT09`** <br>
	This one is base64 but if we decode it we get a base32 string, it is double encoded. <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

3. **[Moderate] `GM4HOU3VHBAW6OKNJJFW6SS2IZ3VAMTYORFDMUC2G44EQULIJI3WIVRUMNCWI6KGK5XEKZDTN5YU2RT2MR3E45KKI5TXSOJTKZJTC4KRKFDWKZTZOF3TORJTGZTXGNKCOE======`**
	Here we add another level, this goes from base32 to base58 to hex to base64
--> REDACTED
<br>

4. **[Hard] `HRBUGQDUHFWDIXKUIBWXIJTHIE3DCY3BIE2FKQSZHNDE6MRUIA2TWWDMHRBV2ZKCHQWFCTLPIE2EEJDBIBZCEW3OHUSTOLRCHNFGMVC6IFJXIQ2AHVMVONSBHVOVIM2MHVPV42J4HQVCQ4REIFHVIJ2WHFWDYQSUHROGILJCIFIU23CXHNCEIXK2HRDVSXKOHV2SQJLC`** <br>
	Keep going with the bases we are now at Base32 > Base85 > Base64 > Base58 > Base85 > Base64 > Base64 <br>
--> REDACTED 
<br>

5. **[Insane]** 
```

```
This one is massive Base32 > Base85 > Decimal > Hex > base91 > base58 > hex > base64 > decimal >hex, super long and boring yet easy to decode.
--> REDACTED

<br/>

### Base and Cipher
In this second part the strings are both encoded and encrypted with some common and easy ciphers.

6. **[Easy] `PJXHQ4S7GEZV6ZTDOZQQ====`** <br>
	This one starts as a base32 string, then we need to decrypt the result using ROT13. <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

7. **[Moderate] `NjZMKVhATl1EcEI2Jio4Q0xuVy1EZSo5ZkFLV0M6QVUtPFpGQ0InIkReYg==`** <br>
	Here there are again multiple encodings: base64 > base85 here we can read that the key is `tango`, now we can Vigenère decode. <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

8. **[Hard] `-!r/X,]n/Z-Zs\X,X$,rI<@]#-9Oh,-=A]R-p9\+`** <br>
	For this one we start with base85 then need to ROT47, now base64 string to base32 and finally ROT13 <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

9. **[Insane]**
```

```
Another quite long one... here we do: base32 > base85 > base91 > decimal > hex > base64 > base58 and finally Vigenère with key `Tangodown`

--> REDACTED

<br/>

### Base, Cipher and Bit Shift
We are now to the last task, here we get both encoding, encryption and some bits are shifted. <br>
To handle the bit shift we can use this website: https://www.dcode.fr/circular-bit-shift

10. **[Moderate] `2D 37 2B 19 31 99 31 B3 B2 AB A5 18 32 37 20 B3 B2 AC 2D 1A 31 B4 A1 3A A4 A3 9C B4 AD 36 AC 9E`** <br>
	 Here we need to start with the bit shift, choose automatic detection, then we go to base64 to conclude with ROT13, with this last one we get multiple results but only one is a logical sentence. <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

11. **[Hard] `19 1A 1C 99 1C 9C A8 38 27 B2 34 27 B9 27 26 28 3C 3B AA 28 A5 AA 2A 29 3C 29 B2 B2 21 B1 AC A6 1C AC 29 A8 38 99 2C`** <br>
	Again start with bit-shift (7), then base58 and ROT13. <br>
--> REDACTED
<br>

12. **[Insane] `A9 A8 2B EE 2A AA C9 C8 C9 AA A8 0F 2A 8A AA EE A9 8A CA A8 A9 4F 6D 46 E9 A8 A8 0F A9 8A 6D 86 A9 AA A9 26 2A 8A 48 E8`** <br>
	For this last one, bit-shift (5) then base64, now again bit-shift  auto (126), base58  and finally conclude with ROT13. <br>
--> REDACTED

<br/>
<br/>

Congratulations, you are now a master at decoding strings!

Hope you had fun following along and completing the challenge.

Catch you in the next CTF 😃
